summary: Completed comprehensive backend testing of Digital Twin Agent installer endpoints and install.cmd UAC self-elevation fix. All 35 tests passed. Verified that the old VBS-based UAC elevation (which caused 'The system cannot find the path specified' and 'Input Error: There is no script file specified' errors) has been completely removed and replaced with a clean PowerShell Start-Process -Verb RunAs approach. The generated install.cmd is syntactically correct, contains all required components, and the download flow works end-to-end.
passed_tests: GET /api/agent/installer/info bundle_contents lists all 3 EXEs (DigitalTwinAgentSetup.exe, agent.exe, uninstaller.exe)
POST /api/agent/installer/download-init returns valid pairing_code in DT-XXXX-XXXX format
install.cmd does NOT contain 'CreateObject' (old VBS remnant)
install.cmd does NOT contain 'cscript' (old VBS remnant)
install.cmd does NOT contain 'ELEV_VBS' (old VBS remnant)
install.cmd does NOT contain 'WScript.Arguments' (old VBS remnant)
install.cmd does NOT contain 'dt-elevate' (old VBS remnant)
install.cmd CONTAINS new PowerShell Start-Process -Verb RunAs elevation
install.cmd contains fsutil dirty query %SYSTEMDRIVE% probe
install.cmd has balanced parentheses (no syntax errors)
install.cmd has no unescaped & outside of REM lines
Auth login still works (dashboard access verified)
